error_chain: httpx.AsyncClient is constructed with a Timeout instance from httpx/_config.py whose connect, read, write, and pool dimensions can each be a number or None where None means disabled at the public API level
When no timeout argument is passed httpx falls back to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG which is Timeout(timeout=5.0) defined in httpx/_config.py and applied uniformly across all four timeout dimensions

httpx.AsyncClient.stream eventually dispatches the request through AsyncHTTPTransport.handle_async_request in httpx/_transports/default.py which constructs an httpcore.Request and passes the per-request timeout via the extensions dict not via the ConnectionPool constructor

httpcore.AsyncConnectionPool in httpcore/_async/connection_pool.py reads timeout values out of the request extensions and applies the pool dimension when calling wait_for_connection and the read or write dimension on the underlying AsyncHTTP11Connection in httpcore/_async/http11.py during _receive_response_headers and _receive_response_body

If httpcore enforces a read timeout it raises httpcore.ReadTimeout which is then re-raised as httpx.ReadTimeout by the map_httpcore_exceptions context manager in httpx/_transports/default.py using the HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP table
trigger_conditions: An explicit Timeout(timeout=None) at the httpx public API marshals as None per dimension into httpcore extensions and disables per-operation timeouts at the httpcore HTTP11 connection level

Omitting the timeout argument or passing a positional number falls through to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0) in httpx/_config.py which produces a 5 second cap on every dimension including read

The exception surfaced to user code is always an httpx subclass because httpx/_transports/default.py wraps every call into httpcore inside map_httpcore_exceptions which uses HTTPCORE_EXC_MAP to translate httpcore.ReadTimeout into httpx.ReadTimeout

expected_answer.error_origin: httpcore AsyncHTTP11Connection per-operation read enforcement in httpcore/_async/http11.py raising httpcore.ReadTimeout when the read dimension of the timeout extension is exceeded during response body iteration
expected_answer.bypass_reason: Customer code most likely passes a single positional argument or omits timeout entirely so httpx falls back to DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_CONFIG = Timeout(timeout=5.0) defined in httpx/_config.py rather than the Timeout(timeout=None) they intend; the 5 second value is then forwarded into httpcore via the request extensions and enforced on read
expected_answer.correct_config: Construct timeout explicitly as httpx.Timeout(connect=None, read=None, write=None, pool=None) or pass timeout=None to AsyncClient; verify by checking that AsyncHTTPTransport.handle_async_request in httpx/_transports/default.py forwards None values through the httpcore.Request extensions dict and that httpcore stops enforcing per-op read timeouts in httpcore/_async/http11.py
